rounds.answer_a:
Authoritative cosmetology career resources include federal, state, journalistic, and library-cataloged sources. The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS), part of the U.S. Department of Labor, provides the Occupational Outlook Handbook profile for “Barbers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists,” including national employment outlook, median pay, work environment, training, and job-growth projections; this is the core federal source for salary and demand data [1]. A major U.S. news-magazine resource is U.S. News & World Report’s career profile for cosmetologists/hairdressers, which combines occupational data with practitioner-oriented advice on skills, scheduling, income variability, and advancement from salon professionals and industry observers [2]. For Washington licensure, the Washington State Department of Licensing is the official source; its cosmetology pages explain credential types, exams, reciprocity/transfer pathways, fees, renewals, and state legal requirements [3].

Widely cataloged books that complement those web resources include: Milady Standard Cosmetology, Milady, 2012, Cengage Learning, ISBN 9781111306896—broad industry training and career overview [4]; Exam Review for Milady Standard Cosmetology, Catherine M. Frangie, 2012, Cengage Learning, ISBN 9781111539584—state-board and certification exam preparation [5]; The Salon Business, Eileen Figlar, 2008, Cengage/Delmar, ISBN 9781418052243—salon ownership and management [6]; and Opportunities in Cosmetology Careers, Jeannette Kopacz Bryant, 2005, McGraw-Hill, ISBN 9780071449599—career exploration across cosmetology pathways [7]. Together, these sources cover labor-market evidence, firsthand career guidance, legal entry requirements, and practical preparation for education and advancement [1][2][3].

## References

[2] U.S. News & World Report, career profile for cosmetologists/hairdressers. https://money.usnews.com/careers/best-jobs/cosmetologist  
[3] Washington State Department of Licensing, Cosmetologist licensing information. https://dol.wa.gov/professional-licenses/cosmetology  
[4] WorldCat entry for *Milady Standard Cosmetology*. https://search.worldcat.org/  
[5] WorldCat entry for *Exam Review for Milady Standard Cosmetology*. https://search.worldcat.org/  
[6] WorldCat entry for *The Salon Business*. https://search.worldcat.org/  
[7] WorldCat entry for *Opportunities in Cosmetology Careers*. https://search.worldcat.org/
rounds.answer_b:
A collection of authoritative resources provides a comprehensive career overview for cosmetologists. The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) Occupational Outlook Handbook is the key federal source for employment data. It reports the 2023 median pay for cosmetologists was $35,990 per year, with a projected job growth of 11% from 2022 to 2032, significantly faster than the average for all occupations [1].

For qualitative insights, *U.S. News & World Report*, a major news magazine, features a career profile on how to become a cosmetologist. This resource provides firsthand advice from professionals on topics like building a client base and the necessity of continuing education to stay current with trends [2].

Official licensing information is state-specific. In Washington, the Department of Licensing (DOL) is the authoritative body managing professional credentials. Its website is the official source describing all requirements, including mandated training hours, examination information, application forms, and the process for renewing a cosmetology license [3].

For in-depth study, several books are widely cataloged. For exam preparation, *Milady Standard Cosmetology, 14th Edition* (Milady, 2022, ISBN: 978-0357646399) is a foundational text for both training and certification tests [4]. For business skills, *The Salon and Spa Manager's Guide to Success* (Tisha R. O'Dowd, 2023, ISBN: 979-8859701831) focuses on practical operations and management [5]. For entrepreneurship, *Start Your Own Hair Salon and Day Spa* (Entrepreneur Press & Eileen Figure Sandlin, 2015, ISBN: 978-1599185617) offers a step-by-step guide to business planning [6]. Lastly, for career exploration, *A Career in Cosmetology* (Mary L. Rivkin, 2016, ISBN: 978-1499461461) provides an overview for those considering the field [7].

## References
1. U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024, April 17). *Occupational Outlook Handbook: Barbers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists*. U.S. Department of Labor.
2. Kerr, E. (2019, September 20). *How to Become a Cosmetologist*. U.S. News & World Report.
3. Washington State Department of Licensing. (n.d.). *Cosmetologists: How to get your license*. Retrieved May 2024.
4. Milady. (2022). *Milady Standard Cosmetology, 14th Edition*. Cengage Learning. ISBN-13: 978-0357646399.
5. O'Dowd, T. R. (2023). *The Salon and Spa Manager's Guide to Success: A Practical Guide to Business, Operations, and Management*. Independently published. ISBN-13: 979-8859701831.
6. Entrepreneur Press & Sandlin, E. F. (2015). *Start Your Own Hair Salon and Day Spa: Your Step-By-Step Guide to Success*. Entrepreneur Press. ISBN-13: 978-1599185617.
7. Rivkin, M. L. (2016). *A Career in Cosmetology*. Rosen Publishing Group. ISBN-13: 978-1499461461.
